title: rhinanthus alpinus subsp. montanus
intro: Rhinanthus alpinus subsp. montanus, also known as the alpine rorippa or mountain bee orchid, is a rare subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae) and is parasitic on other plants, deriving its nutrients by tapping into their vascular systems. Traditionally, Rhinanthus alpinus subsp. montanus has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Rhinanthus alpinus subsp. montanus can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
